name: build_end_to_end
outcome_goal: Make the app builder work end-to-end: type intent, see matching templates, click one, see it render with working modules

jobs.label: build_e2e_flow
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. Use Postgres via $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL (source scripts/_workflow_env.sh && workflow_load_repo_env at repo root). PATH must include /opt/homebrew/bin.

The app builder has:
- React app at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/ (Vite on port 5173, proxies /api to port 8421)
- Dispatch API at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/workflow_api.py (port 8421)
- 24 app templates in Postgres app_manifests table
- 20 quadrant modules registered in src/modules/
- Intent box module that searches /api/templates?q=QUERY and shows clickable results
- QuadrantGrid that renders v2 manifests (version:2, quadrants: {A1: {module, span, config}})

The problem: when you type 'CRM' and click Go, the results appear in the DOM but are hidden by overflow. And the React controlled input doesn't update from preview_fill because onChange doesn't fire.

Fix these issues and make the full flow work:

1. Fix the IntentBoxModule at src/modules/input/IntentBoxModule.tsx:
   - The input onChange must use a proper React state setter (it already does, but the fill tool doesn't trigger it - this is fine for real users)
   - Make the results area ALWAYS visible when there are results - don't let the parent cell hide them
   - Add a 'Browse All' link that shows all 24 templates when clicked (fetches /api/templates with no query)
   - Style the template cards better: name in bold, description below, hover highlight, click navigates

2. Fix the QuadrantGrid CSS at src/grid/QuadrantGrid.css:
   - Remove overflow:hidden that clips content
   - Make rows auto-height so they expand to fit content
   - Ensure the grid looks good at 1400px width with 4 columns

3. Add a simple navigation bar at the top of App.tsx:
   - Show current manifest name
   - 'Home' link that goes to /?manifest=ops-dashboard
   - 'Browse Templates' link that goes to a simple page listing all templates
   - Style: dark bar at top, matches theme

4. Create a landing page manifest in Postgres (id: 'home', name: 'Home'):
   - A1-A4: intent-box spanning full width (4x1) - the main 'What do you want to build?' prompt
   - B1-B4: Empty or a stat-row showing platform stats
   - C1-C2: activity-feed showing recent dispatches
   - C3-C4: registry-browser for exploring components
   - D1-D4: status-grid showing all available templates as clickable cards

5. Make the default manifest 'home' instead of 'dispatch-status' in App.tsx getManifestId()

6. Test by:
   - Connect to Postgres and verify home manifest exists
   - Verify /api/templates returns 24+ results
   - Verify /api/templates?q=CRM returns CRM Dashboard
   - Verify the React app builds without errors: cd surfaces/app && npx tsc --noEmit

Write all changes. Do NOT break existing functionality.
